title: How seriously do you take shipping
display_title: How seriously do you take shipping
wikitext:
[]: How seriously do you take shipping?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Wed Jul 15, 2015 1:45 pm Do you consider it to be serious business or trivial?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jul 15, 2015 1:51 pm I don't ever think of it as "serious business". It's people taking characters that they think should be in a relationship and putting them together through fanfiction or fanart-- I couldn't think of it as being anything but trivial, really.

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Wed Jul 15, 2015 1:54 pm You'd be surprised after what iv'e seen on the EVA Reddit page

[]:             Quote Post by Iuvenal » Wed Jul 15, 2015 2:27 pm Arcadia's legacy wrote: You'd be surprised after what iv'e seen on the EVA Reddit page

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jul 15, 2015 2:54 pm Arcadia's legacy wrote: You'd be surprised after what iv'e seen on the EVA Reddit page

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Wed Jul 15, 2015 3:05 pm That thread asks the big question. This thread asks the simple question

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 15, 2015 3:06 pm I love what I wrote here regarding stepmom Rits so I'm just gonna link to it: https://forum.evageeks.org/post/787184/Is-there-a-deeper-reason-we-pick-our-ships/#787184

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Wed Jul 15, 2015 11:17 pm Oh man when I was a younger teenager I took it super seriously, like "I will only read AxS fan fiction" seriously. Now its just one of those things that is nice to think about and almost nostalgic to remember the times when people would get into horrifying flame wars about this stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 16, 2015 1:20 am LRS fics should have tracking numbers so I know when my shipping will arrive.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 16, 2015 1:29 am I take shipping in that playful, funny, 5-year-old mashing a Ken and Barbie doll together in the face sorta way. Especially Mari x Asuka. Any more seriously and it takes all the fun out of it for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Jul 16, 2015 1:46 am I go back and forth between caring immensely to not caring at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Seoul Gamer » Thu Jul 16, 2015 3:07 am There isn't really anything to take seriously. The only plausible ships are all well and truly sunk in the actual show.

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Thu Jul 16, 2015 7:45 am To be fair, there does seem to be a small slither of hope for SxA in the final scene

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Jul 16, 2015 12:17 pm I think it's cute. As a hobby. But it's when people start to criticize & speak ill of the original or new official works in the ongoing story BECAUSE it doesn't support a person's favorite ship that I start attacking it viciously. I also despise it when people start attaching it to their identity because that's just sad.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 16, 2015 1:16 pm The real question is how seriously does one take shipping and handling.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 16, 2015 1:25 pm Seoul Gamer wrote: There isn't really anything to take seriously. The only plausible ships are all well and truly sunk in the actual show.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 16, 2015 1:43 pm Shipping (SxA) was a lot more serious in the early days of the site, enough that my "but there's no there, there" stance got me labelled as a notorious anti-shipper. Today, things have mellowed a lot.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Jul 16, 2015 3:42 pm It's rare; but there are even sites that forbid shipping! Yes, that's the policy at the QC forums.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaudSept » Thu Jul 16, 2015 6:02 pm I really don't take it seriously, I also mainly don't care. Though when the anime is trully about love and stuff (or when it is a good part of it), it's appropriate to concider it! However, crossover pairing... I just find it pointless. Shinji x Eren Yes it's a thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Jul 16, 2015 7:09 pm Not seriously at all. Fanfics are good for exploring the possibilities, though. But, honestly, Eva itself isn't about romance. If you want romance, watch Kare Kano. Other than that, it's bullship.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jul 15, 2015 1:51 pm I don't ever think of it as "serious business". It's people taking characters that they think should be in a relationship and putti
